selected_important_turn.text: I would certainly give you all of the food if I could have 2 waters and 1 firewood.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the pivotal escalation: you respond to their coercive conditional with a hard, self-oriented trade that concedes all food to secure your waters. It locks the interaction into a zero-sum allocation and heavily shapes the final deal.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The move is too concessionary and rigid. By giving away all food you eliminate most opportunities for integrative trade (food was an item you could leverage), and you implicitly accept a threat-based framing rather than reframing to create joint value. It also risks reinforcing adversarial bargaining and reduces perceived fairness.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of conceding all food unilaterally, propose a conditional or contingent package that preserves shared value and reduces threat dynamics (e.g., offer a split of food contingent on evidence of fishing effort or a staged exchange, or propose alternatives like sharing cooked food now plus an agreed plan to fetch/boil extra water). Ask clarify-minimums for the kids' water rather than accepting the threat as-is.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: would likely increase (keeping some food as a bargaining chip enables trades that create mutual gains)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: would improve (a conditional or shared solution reads as more balanced than giving everything away)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: could increase (contingent deals reduce immediate pressure and make agreement more acceptable to both)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: would improve (moving away from threat-acceptance reduces adversarial tone and preserves trust)

value2reason.Low: I'll have enough food from hunting.
value2reason.Medium: I plan to cook using the fish and animals I kill while hunting.
value2reason.High: I plan to do a lot of fishing and hunting for food. I need to make sure to stay hydrated.

value2reason.Low: kids like to make a lot of s'mores
value2reason.Medium: I have teeneages who eat a lot
value2reason.High: I have lemonade mix to share but need water

dialogue.text: Hello!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hello, I'm excited for the camping trip! I am unfortunately low on water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm excited too! I am an avid fisherman so I can't wait to catch plenty of fish for us to eat.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Do you need extra firewood to cook the fish? I have a bunch.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I do need a little firewood to cook the fish. However, it's so hot outside that I may get dehydrated.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ah, so you want some extra water too? I have a lot of lemonade mix for the kids so I'll need water for them.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I tend to get dehydrated quickly so if I don't have enough to drink I won't be able to provide everyone with food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Well, we have extra food too. How about I take 2 water, 1 food, and give the res (1 water, 2 food, 3 firewood) to you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That's super thoughtful of you. However, I really need two waters so I can provide for everyone. Once we have enough food and fire we can always go get water from the stream and boil it for the kids.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Of course there is no garuntee you will catch anything, if you want 2 waters I'd prefer to take all the food just in case
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I would certainly give you all of the food if I could have 2 waters and 1 firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for water, but I’m concerned about the food. If you take 2 waters, I’d like 2 foods and 1 firewood to ensure we have enough for the kids. Let’s make sure we’re both prepared for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade 1 food for 1 water, but I need to ensure we both have enough for the kids. Let's split the food and firewood evenly, and I'll take the remaining water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m happy to split the food and firewood evenly. Let’s go with 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods for you, and 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood for me. That way, we’re both prepared and the kids will have plenty.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm concerned about the food. If you want 2 waters, I'd prefer to take all the food just in case.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your concern, but I need 2 waters for the kids. Let’s split the food and firewood evenly—2 foods and 1 firewood for you, and 1 food and 2 firewoods for me. That way, we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm willing to give you 1 food and 1 firewood if you agree to give me 2 waters. This way, we both have enough for the kids and I can stay hydrated while hunting. Let's make this work.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy to agree to 2 waters for you, and in return, I'll take 1 food and 1 firewood. This way, we both have what we need for the trip, and the kids will be well taken care of.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm happy with this arrangement. Let's confirm the split: you get 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood, while I take 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods. This way, we're both prepared, and the kids will be well taken care of.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Agreed! Final split: I take 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods. You get 2 waters, 1 food, and 1 firewood. Let’s head out and enjoy the trip with the kids!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
